This podcast brings together three accomplished women in healthcare who have not only navigated complex systems as professionals, but have also stepped beyond them—building companies, leading ventures, and redefining what it means to be entrepreneurs. With roots in different cultures and experiences shaped by resilience, they offer a perspective that blends clinical expertise, business insight, and lived reality.


Each episode dives into real conversations about leadership, innovation, identity, and the challenges of building something meaningful from the ground up. From navigating healthcare inequities to scaling businesses and balancing cultural expectations, nothing is off the table.


Grounded in knowledge and driven by purpose, this podcast is a space for learning, connection, and empowerment—created for communities who don’t always see themselves reflected in traditional narratives, but are shaping the future anyway.

title: Credit FC
author: Sydney
description:
Most football coverage stops at the transfer fee. Credit FC starts where the real story begins, the capital behind the club.


Each week, one deal gets torn down to its structure: who's lending, against what collateral, on what terms, and what happens when the cash flow doesn't show up. An acquisition, a refinancing, a default, a stadium bond — pulled apart the way a credit desk would look at it, not the way a match report covers it.


No hot takes, no rumor-mill. Just a clear read on whether the money makes sense, and what it tells you about which clubs survive their ambitions and which become cautionary tales.


If you own a club, lend to one, or just want to understand why the smart money moves the way it does, this is your desk.

title: The Purist Peptides Science Lab
description:
The Purist Peptides Science Lab is a research-focused podcast created for curious listeners who want a clearer understanding of peptide science, laboratory quality, and the role of precision in modern research. Each episode explores a different corner of the peptide world, from purity standards and compound stability to research categories, product selection, and the broader science behind cellular signaling and metabolic pathways.

At the center of every conversation is Purist Peptides, a brand built around uncompromising quality, analytical verification, and a science-first approach that supports informed research discussions.The series is designed to educate, not hype. Instead of vague promises, each episode breaks down how peptides are positioned in research contexts, why testing and consistency matter, and how scientists and educated consumers evaluate compounds responsibly.

The goal is to make the subject approachable while still respecting the technical depth of the field. Whether the listener is exploring longevity research, recovery-focused compounds, sleep support categories, or metabolic pathways, Purist Peptides serves as the reference point for a clearer, more disciplined conversation about precision research compounds.

title: Unpublished
author: Angela Mattes and Daniel Stevens
description:
Follow aspiring author Angela Mattes as she drags her fictionally illiterate friend and cohost Daniel Stevens along on the long, bewildering, and occasionally tear-soaked journey of writing a book on the podcast Unpublished.


Angela may be unpublished, but she is not beginning from a blank page. She has already completed one original manuscript and written hundreds of thousands of words of fiction read by hundreds of thousands of people online. Along the way, she has developed a process for getting past the obstacles that keep so many aspiring writers from ever beginning: writer’s block, disorganization, self-doubt, too many competing ideas, and the overwhelming question of where to start.


As she writes her next manuscript, Angela will break down how she turns the fragments floating around in her head into characters, story arcs, outlines, scenes, chapters, and eventually a finished book. Daniel brings an outsider’s perspective, an addiction to Wikipedia, and the questions of someone who has never understood what writers are actually doing behind their laptops.


The journey begins with Eros et Flamingo, Angela’s literary novel about a heartsick assassin who has fallen for one too many of her marks and devises a slightly unhinged plan to find someone capable of loving her back.


Part writing diary, part practical guide, part behind-the-scenes look at the publishing process, and part conversation between two friends, Unpublished follows the creativity, uncertainty, obsession, rejection, breakthroughs, and inevitable sobbing involved in turning a flurry of ideas into a fully realized manuscript.



The book has not been written yet.

There is no literary agent. 

Publication is far from guaranteed.


For now, it's Unpublished.

The metaphor of the "back 9" in golf is used to convey the idea of a second chance in life, with fresh opportunities to achieve something remarkable. I'm so grateful you're here! I'm Anna Casey, a mom of 2, military spouse, and lover of deep conversations.

I started this podcast because I know what its like to finish a long day of solo parenting to pop in your headphones and feel immediately less alone and more anchored.

I also know the feeling of being completely overwhelmed at the thought of "holding down the ship" while your spouse leaves again.

But I also know when we come together and learn from each other and share our stories, we are better for it.

On Long Distance Friends, you'll hear from a range of experts and friends on all the things that build us up to handle the hardships of life - from motherhood, mental health, community-building, resilience, faith/spirituality, and more.

Every conversation is laced with honesty, curiosity, open-mindedness, and grace.

I hope you find yourself feeling at home, like you're sitting right here on the couch with us. Even if you're thousands of miles away.

We can do this, together, even if at a distance.

Anna Casey

IG, FB, Tiktok: @ldf.pod

Fun fact - this show is produced entirely during my kids nap time! If you love these conversations, please consider writing a review and actually pressing "subscribe/follow"! This is the #1 way to help this show gain momentum and help these conversations and get them into more women's hands.

Welcome to That’s Outlandishly Hot.


It’s still us: Nikki and Lisa, the hosts you know and love from An Outlandishly Hot Recap Show and Blood of My Blood: An Outlandishly Hot Recap Show.


We’re back with the same unhinged enthusiasm for Outlander, the same questionable theories, the same emotional spirals over fictional Scotsmen, and the same inability to stay on topic.

But now we’re making room for some of the things you’ve been asking for in our DMs: more recommendations, more behind-the-scenes stories, more personal tangents, more special episodes on the latest smut taking over the internet, the female gaze, and the TV shows everyone can't stop talking about, plus more of whatever weird internet rabbit hole we’ve fallen down this week.

title: Breath of Life Stories
author: Lisa Bailey-Brown
description:
Most parents never imagine that a single mistake or oversight could change their child's life forever. Lewis and Zoe Steeper share their heart breaking journey from the tragic loss of their nine-month-old Oliver to a powerful mission: turning grief into life-saving impact. Their story exposes the raw realities of a preventable choking accident, the hospital's heart  breaking response, and the relentless fight to honor Oliver’s memory through innovative safety initiatives.
In this candid episode, you’ll discover the shocking details of the incident how a nursery’s failure and a simple meal turned into a life-altering tragedy and learn about the critical importance of food safety, supervision, and preparedness. Lewis reveals how their tragedy propelled them into founding the Oliver Steeper Foundation, which is transforming nursery safety with LifeVac devices, training thousands across the UK, and fighting to prevent other families from enduring similar pain.

We break down not only the emotional aftermath but also the practical steps parents and caregivers can take to avoid such tragedies. You’ll hear about the ground breaking work of the Oliver Steeper Foundation, from fundraising efforts to nationwide device distribution, and how their advocacy is saving lives sometimes within minutes. This episode emphasizes that even the smallest act like learning first aid or insisting on food safety can mean the difference between loss and survival.
Why does this matter? Because choking incidents can happen to anyone, at any age, regardless of safety assurances. 

Lewis and Zoe’s story is a call to action for every parent, nursery worker, and caregiver to prioritize life-saving skills and tools. Their relentless dedication to keeping Oliver’s legacy alive is inspiring a nationwide change, giving hope and practical knowledge to families everywhere.
Perfect for parents, childcare professionals, and anyone who wants to make a tangible difference your awareness and actions today could save a child’s life tomorrow. Join Lewis and Zoe in their mission to turn tragedy into hope, and be part of the movement that’s changing countless lives across the UK.

 Lewis Steeper is co-founder of the Oliver Steeper Foundation, a charity committed to preventing childhood choking tragedies through innovative safety initiatives and community education. His family’s story is a heartfelt reminder that every life matters and that collective awareness can forge a safer future for children everywhere.

author: Mojisola Sapara
description: Who are you now — after the role, the season, or the version of you everyone knew no longer fits? Who Am I Now? is a podcast about identity, faith, growth, and reinvention. Honest conversations about knowing whose you are and owning who you are. New episodes twice a month.

title: For the Fruitful
author: Alicia Lourdes Watson
description: Welcome to For the Fruitful, a faith-filled podcast for women seeking to grow deeper in their relationship with God while navigating real life with wisdom, purpose, and grace. Through a biblical lens and the perspective of a woman of God, this podcast explores faith, relationships, culture, personal growth, identity, calling, and everyday challenges with honesty and encouragement.

title: Sénior Huevo - Spanish Rugby Podcast
author: Sénior Huevo
description: Giving you all the insight and taking you inside Spanish Rugby Union

title: Small Business Showcase
author: B-rad
description: Small Business Showcase, hosted by B-rad, gives local businesses, creators, and community leaders a real stage to share who they are, what they do, and why they do it. Sometimes the old ways are the best, and real conversations still matter.

title: Mer and Oz
author: Mer and Oz
description: We will probably talk about anything, but we can't promise it will be good or correct.

title: YT Manual Video Test
author: Michael
description: See title

title: Editing Photos with Hyris
description: "Editing Photos with Hyris" is a podcast about photo editing tips, creative techniques, and visual enhancement. In this episode, we also explore useful Hypic features and editing ideas to help create stunning and professional-looking photos.

title: Being Unmothered
author: Unmothered Daughter
description:
“Being unmothered” describes people like us—those born to a mother who simply wasn’t. 
We all have different stories. This is mine. 
I hope that as you listen, you’ll see parts of yourself and know you are not alone. One of the hardest parts of being unmothered is the deep loneliness nothing seems to fill, but knowing you are not alone helps with that.
This is not a story of hate, but of love and abandonment. This limited series is “Being Unmothered.” And I am your host, Lyla.
This is not a story of hate, but of love and abandonment. This limited series is “Being Unmothered.” And I am yo

title: Greece Travel Host
author: Greece Travel Cast
description: Master your Greek vacation planning with the Greece Travel Cast. Focused on practical travel organization, insider tips, and logistics, this podcast is designed to take the guesswork out of your Mediterranean holiday. Discover curated itineraries, hidden coastal destinations, and expert advice to ensure your summer travel is smooth, memorable, and stress-free. Hit subscribe and let’s map out your perfect Greek journey together!

title: Service Gas Heater Adelaide
description: Advantage Gas Services provides professional service gas heater Adelaide solutions for residential and commercial properties. Our experienced gas fitters specialise in gas heater service, gas heater services, gas heater servicing, gas heater repairs, ducted gas heating maintenance, carbon monoxide testing, and gas leak detection. We ensure safe, efficient gas heating and heating system performance. Trust our qualified gasfitter team for servicing gas heater, servicing gas heaters, and service gas heaters across Adelaide.

title: The Luxe Lounge Podcast
author: Key
description:
Welcome to The Luxe Lounge Podcast, where real conversations inspire real growth.
Hosted by Key, this is your safe space for honest discussions about healing, relationships, self-discovery, mindset, personal growth, and navigating life's highs and lows.
Every episode is designed to leave you feeling seen, inspired, and empowered. Whether we're talking about overcoming obstacles, building confidence, embracing change, or simply learning to become the best version of ourselves, you'll find authentic conversations with no judgment and no filters.
So grab your favorite drink, get comfortable, and pull up a seat in The Luxe Lounge.
Because healing starts where honesty begins.
New episodes every Sunday.

title: On Second Thought with Jackie Pfeifer
author: Jackie Pfeifer
description:
Welcome to On Second Thought with me, Jackie Pfeifer, a podcast for ambitious women and mothers who built a life doing everything "right," and are now questioning whether it's actually the life they want to keep living.
Nothing has to be falling apart for you to question what comes next. You can be grateful for your life and still change your mind on the direction you want to take.
Through honest conversations, new perspectives, and practical tools, this podcast will help you feel brave enough to question yourself, honest enough to get real clarity, and capable enough to explore new directions without blowing up everything you've built.
Because sometimes creating the life you want starts with one honest realization: "On second thought, I want something different."

title: Women in the Bible--She Walked So You Could Run
author: Linda Beverett
description: Across the pages of Scripture, ordinary women were asked to do extraordinary things. They walked through barrenness, betrayal, exile, and impossible odds — not as perfect heroes, but as real women with real fears who chose faith anyway. Their stories aren't dusty history. They're a living inheritance. Every tear they cried, every step they took in the dark, every door they walked through was for YOU. Because they walked — you don't have to stay where you are. Because they endured — you can run into your purpose with clarity and courage.

title: The Nashville Post Podcast
author: FW Publishing
description: The Nashville Post Podcast covers the business industries and people making moves in Middle Tennessee. Hosted by Post Editor-in-Chief Nicolle Praino, the interviews feature a range of C-suite executives to start-up founders. Listen now to get smart, relevant and engaging insights on topics such as finance, technology, development and more.

title: Couch to Cardio Podcast
author: Crystal Gareau
description:
Trying to get our life together… but also we love snacks and binge-worthy TV.
Welcome to Couch to Cardio — a cozy lifestyle podcast hosted by Crystal about rebuilding routines, wellness, workouts, grief, motivation, and millennial life without pretending to have it all together. Expect relatable conversations, treadmill TV binges, Starbucks frappes, realistic fitness talk, and cozy chaos.
New episodes weekly for the girls healing, growing, and pressing “next episode.”

title: Open Flame
author: Fiamma
description:
Open Flame is a podcast about becoming.
Hosted by Fiamma, an Italian-British architecture student living in Edinburgh, each episode explores creativity, identity, cities, films, design, relationships, ambition, and all the uncertainty that comes with your twenties.

title: The Sheer Growth Podcast Season 1
author: The Sheer Growth Podcast
description: Welcome to The Sheer Growth Podcast - Season 1, where host James Sheerin sits down with founders, agency leaders, marketers, and innovators to explore how AI is transforming business growth. Discover practical strategies, real-world success stories, client acquisition tactics, automation insights, and the future of work. Whether you're an entrepreneur, agency owner, or business leader, you'll gain actionable ideas to grow smarter, scale faster, and stay ahead in the AI-driven economy.

title: Duchess After Dark
author: Duchess of NJ
description: Musings from the Duchess of NJ

title: Mad Seasoned Podcast
author: Life Dutchee
description: Hosted by Life Dutchee. Real conversations about business, fatherhood, music, culture, current events, and the lessons learned through experience.

title: Doubt Diaries
author: Pippa Woodhead
description: If you often think things like: 'am I good enough?', 'I'm not ready yet', 'what if I do it wrong?', welcome to Doubt Diaries. A podcast about self-doubt and the people you'd never expect to have it. Host Pippa Woodhead has 2,000+ interviews behind her with CEOs, Olympians and Ivy League psychologists. The surprise: most of the experts told her self-doubt can actually be good for us. As Harvard's Dr Ellen Langer put it: 'If you don't have doubt, you're mindless.' Past guests include Wim Hof, Tim Shriver, Nir Eyal, Aisha Bowe, and Dr Ellen Langer. Archive available now. New episodes summer 2026.

title: Please Look Up
author: Particle WA
description: Tune in each month for a guided tour of the night sky as seen from Perth, Western Australia. Each episode highlights all of the fascinating constellations, planets, and asteroids that you should be keeping an eye out for in the night sky. And don't worry, we'll also be highlighting the latest in space news so you'll always be in the know when it comes to the extra-terrestrial. Please Look Up is brought to you by https://particle.scitech.org.au/ (Particle) and powered by http://scitech.org.au/ (Scitech).

title: Better Campus Extension Community
author: David Warner
description: Join the Better Campus Extension Community for simple Canvas tips, helpful updates, and smarter ways to improve your learning experience.

title: Geeko Station
author: geekostation
description:
Welcome to Geeko Station, the official podcast for engineers, architects, and IT decision-makers navigating the future of open source. Join us each week for a sometimes irreverent, occasionally snarky, always insightful look beyond the headlines as we wind down the week, and explore the critical intersection of enterprise Linux, Kubernetes, and the rapidly evolving world of Artificial Intelligence.

We break down the latest shifts in cloud-native security, software supply chain integrity, and infrastructure automation—minus the dry corporate jargon. Whether you are scaling workloads at the edge, integrating AI-assisted workflows into your development lifecycle, or just trying to survive your latest deployment, Geeko Station provides the practical, actionable insights you need to build smarter, safer, and more resilient systems.

Think of it as your Friday afternoon tech sync, with a lot more honesty and a lot less PowerPoint. If it’s driving the open-source community forward, it’s happening at the station.

title: Hot Words in China
author: eShyen
description:
Every language has words that can't quite be translated. But in China, those words don't just resist translation — they carry entire worlds inside them.


Hot Words in China is a podcast that takes one Chinese internet buzzword per episode and unpacks what's really hiding underneath. The humour, the history, the quiet frustration, the collective spirit of a generation living through rapid, relentless change.


Hosted by eShyen, a Chinese native who grew up from village to city, studied in the UK, and worked for five years navigating cross-cultural business between the two countries. She didn't study these words from the outside. She lived inside them.


One word. One episode. A whole world behind it.

title: Patrick Andraous
author: Patrick Andraous
description: Career growth becomes more achievable with Patrick Andraous providing practical coaching, career planning, and personal branding strategies. He helps professionals recognize their strengths, prepare for new opportunities, and build confidence during career transitions. His supportive approach empowers individuals to make informed decisions, develop valuable skills, and achieve long-term professional success with greater clarity and purpose.

title: The Beach Vacation
description: The beach vacation was supposed to be an adventure, a sun drenched escape packed into the back of a wood paneled station wagon. But for seven year old Amanda, the memory of it was a blurred collage of bright light and sudden terror.

title: Pacific Air HVAC
description: Welcome to Pacific Air HVAC Company New Jersey, where we blend family values with professional service. Since our inception in 2009, we've remained a proudly family-owned and operated company. It all started with Ali and his trusty 1994 Ford Econoline Cargo van. From those humble beginnings, we've blossomed into a thriving enterprise boasting a fleet of 6 vehicles and a dedicated team of 15+ workers. Our journey has been marked by a commitment to excellence in every aspect of our service. We owe our growth to our unwavering attention to customer requests and our relentless pursuit of top-notch customer service. As we continue to expand, our core values remain unchanged, ensuring that every interaction with Pacific Air is marked by professionalism, reliability, and care. Pacific Air is a leading HVAC provider serving North Jersey for over 20 years. We offer a range of HVAC services including repair, installation, and maintenance for both residential and commercial customers. Our team is comprised of experienced and certified technicians who are committed to delivering the highest level of service and ensuring your complete satisfaction. We use the most advanced technology and techniques to provide you with the best possible service while maintaining competitive pricing. ​Pacific Air has been providing top-quality heating, air conditioning, air quality, and commercial services for years. Our specialities include preventive maintenance, repairs, and installations, all backed by our 100% satisfaction guarantee. Our team of experts is highly trained and certified, and they are always improving their skills to stay on top of the latest technologies and trends in our industry. Contact us today and let us help you keep your home or business comfortable and safe all year-round.

title: The Angry Ginger Podcast
author: Angry Ginger Podcast
description: "Life, laughs, and lessons from a slightly irritated redhead."

title: The Hegemony Horror
author: thehegemonyhorror
description:
In the realm of Elamir, a half elf demon hunter named Delora traverses Castle Hegemony to rescue High King Ivar and stop a demonic invasion. This seasoned warrior will be tested by adversaries that range from abominations with multiple heads, hellspawn of all shapes and sizes, sorcerers who control gargoyles, inhuman shadows with incredible strength and hatred, and terrifying nightmares of her past and present. Will this dangerous mission earn Delora her long desired knighthood or is she destined to become a monster unworthy of honor? 
This dark fantasy audio drama contains sounds of violence, horror, and foul language. Listener discretion is advised. 
The Hegemony Horror is a full cast production written, edited, and directed by William Nunn

title: Between Worlds with Jack Sorrin
author: Jack Sorrin
description:
Between Worlds with Jack Sorin is a weekly podcast for thoughtful people trying to stay sane, clear, and human in a world that feels louder, faster, stranger, and more divided by the day.

Jack Sorin explores the forces shaping modern life — attention, technology, AI, politics, trust, status, meaning, beauty, belonging, and the quiet ache of not fitting the script.

This is not rage-bait. It is not recruiting. It is not self-help fluff. It is cultural diagnosis with human warmth: a weekly map and a weekly move for people who want intellectual depth, practical clarity, and language for the things they feel but cannot quite name.

If you are overwhelmed by the noise, suspicious of easy answers, and still stubbornly interested in truth, hope, and a more humane way to live, you are in the right place.

No yelling. No recruiting. Just clarity with teeth.

title: Keep Brookies Common
author: Elijah
description:
Keep Brookies Common is a podcast about the brook trout, a unique trout species native to the East Coast of North America. With populations declining across its current range, this podcast aims on raising awareness about the Brook trout, the challenges it faces, and what we can do to help!


Produced in association with the Ecological Society of America for the Science Communication in the Parks project, your host, Elijah Edwards, an outdoor educator in Charlottesville, Virginia, will guide you through the wonderful world of brook trout through interviews with experts.


Stay tuned for updates with new episodes!

title: MO State global talks
author: MO State global talks
description: Welcome to MO State Global Talks, brought to you by Missouri State University. Hosted by Madhusudan, a graduate student from Nepal, we share stories and experiences from our global community. In each episode, we explore student life and the moments that shape their journey, highlighting our sponsored students and their unique paths. If you have any feedback or would like to connect, feel free to email us at mostateglobaltalks@gmail.com

title: Moshpit & Mental Health
author: Jules
description:
Safe Place. Stay Heavy! 
Welcome to Moshpit & Mental Health, the international podcast where the loud world of rock, metal, punk, and hardcore meets the vulnerable reality of psychological well-being.
This podcast is hosted by a social psychiatric specialist who is also a dedicated concertgoer and fan.
Taking deep dives into iconic lyrics, solo episodes with professional coping insights, and raw conversations with musicians and fans.
Because screaming along to heavy music is liberating —but you shouldn't have to face the heavy stuff in your head all alone. #mentalhealthrocks

title: Trent's Last Case: A Philip Trent Mystery
description: In the quaint English seaside village of St. Loo, a chilling murder disrupts the calm. Sigsbee Manderson, a powerful yet unpopular financial tycoon, is found dead just steps from his own home. As journalist-turned-detective Philip Trent arrives to cover the story for a London newspaper, he quickly realizes this case is far from ordinary. With a cast of intriguing characters and hidden motives, Trent must navigate the complexities of wealth, power, and betrayal to uncover the truth. Will he solve the mystery before the pressure mounts? Join Trent on a gripping journey filled with twists, as he peels back the layers of deception in a town where nothing is as it seems. This classic detective fiction by Edmund Clerihew Bentley promises suspense and sharp wit, making it a must-listen for fans of whodunits and literary mysteries alike.

title: TM SIM Help Center
description: Simple guides, fixes, and updates for TM SIM registration, status checks, and common registration issues in the Philippines.

title: Office Hours with Gabby Starnes
author: Gabby Starnes
description:
Marketing shouldn't feel like a guessing game.


Yet every day, business owners, entrepreneurs, and marketers are told to post more content, chase the latest trends, and spend money on tactics that often fail to create real results. The outcome? More stress, more confusion, and revenue that feels impossible to predict.


Welcome to Office Hours with Gabby Starnes—the podcast that helps you turn marketing from a source of anxiety into a predictable growth engine for your business.


Hosted by Gabby Starnes, Owner & CEO of the marketing agency Helps2 and growth strategist with more than 17 years of marketing experience, this show breaks down what actually works when it comes to generating leads, building authority, increasing sales, and creating sustainable business growth.


Each episode delivers practical insights, real-world strategies, honest conversations, and actionable marketing lessons you can implement immediately—without the fluff, jargon, or overwhelming complexity.


Whether you're a small business owner, nonprofit leader, creative entrepreneur, or marketing professional, you'll learn how to create marketing systems that attract the right people, convert them into customers, and drive predictable revenue.


Because marketing was never meant to be random.

It's meant to build predictable growth.


Welcome to Office Hours with Gabby Starnes
Real Marketing That Builds Predictable Growth.

title: Simple, NOT Easy
author: michellejack
description:
What if your next coffee break wasn’t just a bitch session…but a bold, gritty, real exchange you could actually act on....Today?
Welcome to Simple, Not Easy, where we talk about the flow of work in the wild. You choose how you show up, how you function within the team, and the environments we create together.
Cyndee the insightful illuminator…guiding you to see what’s really going on, with compassion and accountability.
Michelle the bold black marker…calling it like it is and challenging you to own it.
This isn’t theory. It is in the real-world application where risk is real, pace is undeniable and decisions matter.
We challenge you to experiment and amplify your impact, your human intelligence.
So grab your coffee, get intentional…and let’s make work simple, especially when it’s not easy.

title: Uncommon Sense with Common Terry
author: Common Terry
description:
Common Terry has done it all: fought for freedom via the US Army, lost his freedom courtesy of the government he served, moved from NY to TX only to experience poverty, divorce, love, loss, heartbreak and triumph...and a couple misunderstandings. Now he plans to take those stories and more to the comedy stage. No topic is untouchable, and no soul immune, he's speaking on anything that can be put into words.


Expect a chaotic yet brilliant blend of hard-earned wisdom, unfiltered sarcasm, and unhinged rants. No subject is safe, no filter is applied, and no punch is pulled.


Tune in for the most brutally honest perspective you didn't know you needed.

title: Japan morning Commute
author: Japan Morning Commute
description: This is a lo-fi podcast in which a 45 year-old man living in Japan goes to work. His 40-minute walk/15-minute cycle/10-minute car journey will be documented on a daily basis. Expect descriptions of life in Japan, anecdotes, surprise encounters, whimsy, wit, complaints, and above all, a very slow pace. This is not a high-octane podcast, it is a companion. So settle back and listen to Joe blabber on about nothing much over the sounds of the outdoors and worryingly heavy breathing.

title: African Australians Conversations
author: SESSION in PROGRESS
description:
African Australians' Conversations is a podcast amplifying the voices, stories, and contributions of African Australians. 
Through honest conversations, it explores identity, migration, culture, and community impact while challenging stereotypes and reshaping narratives. From business owners to creatives and community leaders, each episode reveals lived experiences that often go unheard. 
This podcast invites listeners to better understand multicultural Australia through real stories, connection, and shared humanity.

title: John Thorndyke's Cases: The Master Detective's Investigations
description: In this captivating collection, detective John Thorndyke tackles a series of intriguing cases that challenge his sharp mind and keen instincts. R. Austin Freeman weaves together tales filled with clever deductions and unexpected twists, showcasing Thorndyke's unique forensic skills. Each story immerses listeners in a world of mystery, where logic and science play crucial roles in solving crimes. As he navigates through complex clues and puzzling evidence, Thorndyke's character shines with intellect and determination. This collection not only entertains but also invites listeners to engage their own reasoning abilities. Fans of classic detective fiction will appreciate Freeman's masterful storytelling and the rich atmosphere of early 20th-century crime-solving. Whether you’re a longtime admirer of the genre or new to detective stories, these cases promise to keep you on the edge of your seat, eager to unravel each mystery alongside Thorndyke. Discover the art of deduction and the thrill of the chase in this timeless anthology.

title: Certifiably Awkward
author: Gabriela & Amy & Lena
description: Hosted by Gabriela, Amy, and Lena, three friends bonded by cringe-worthy moments and embarrassing stories. When people kept saying, “You should do a podcast—I’d listen,” they decided to take the hint. So they grabbed some microphones and got to work.

title: Sportscard Recollecting Podcast
author: matthewlycans
description: My look at sports card recollecting after 30 years out of the hobby.  The learnings I have discovered while trying to start back into collecting and figure out exploding hobby.

title: Sagittarius Sidequests
author: Kloee Taylor and Elizabeth
description: Sagittarius Sidequests is an astrology podcast hosted by Kloee and Elizabeth, two Sagittarius Risings with plenty of opinions and absolutely no shortage of tangents. From birth charts and transits to astrology hot takes and personal stories, each episode explores the cosmos through curiosity, humor, and real-life experiences. Think of it as a conversation between friends who love astrology and aren't afraid to challenge the usual narratives.

title: Concrete Driveway Installation Orlando
description: Concrete driveway installation in Orlando is a durable and practical solution for homeowners looking to enhance both the appearance and functionality of their property.

title: Marty Lawrence EP1
author: Marty Lawrence
description:
This is a self-development experiment, just happening in public.
Unfiltered thoughts, weekly reflections, and a willingness to say things before they’re perfectly formed.
It might not always be polished or insightful and that’s kind of the point.
I’m opening the door to feedback and criticism, because that’s where real growth comes from.
Not just becoming a better runner but a better communicator, partner, and person.

title: The Art of Living by Design
author: Leisha Segboer & Dwayne Tucker
description: The Art of Living by Design is a podcast exploring what becomes possible when we stop living by default and start living by design. Join Leisha Segboer and Dwayne Tucker as they dive into personal growth, spirituality, conscious living, financial freedom, relationships, wellness, and the practical tools needed to create a life aligned with your values, purpose, and highest potential.

title: tree trimming moncton
description: Tree trimming Moncton is an important part of maintaining healthy, attractive, and safe landscapes. Regular trimming helps remove dead or damaged branches, encourages healthy growth, and reduces the risk of falling limbs during storms or harsh winter weather.

title: Black Forward
author: KZFR 90.1FM & Black Resiliency Project
description:
Black Forward is a podcast produced by the Black Resiliency Project in partnership with KZFR 90.1 FM through the Chico Speaks initiative.

Across five conversations, Black Forward centers the voices, stories, and lived experiences of Black community members in Northern California. The series explores reentry, recovery, entrepreneurship, elder wisdom, youth leadership, and the community-rooted work needed to move forward with dignity, resilience, and purpose.

Grounded in the mission of the Black Resiliency Project, Black Forward uplifts marginalized voices while highlighting culturally responsive care, harm reduction, economic empowerment, civic engagement, and generational resilience.

This series invites listeners to reflect on the barriers Black communities continue to face, the solutions already being built, and the power of Black-led community support.

title: Shopless
author: Shopless Podcast
description: Welcome to Shopless, where we engage with industry leaders in retail and commerce media to uncover their journeys to success. As many of us know, there's no formal education path specifically for retail media. Our goal is to explore the diverse career paths that have led these individuals to their current positions and share valuable insights along the way.

title: Wild engineers
author: Melanie Farmer
description: Wild Engineers explores how nature solves the functions engineers care about most: regulating temperature, managing flow, strengthening structures, protecting from fire, and more. Hosted by Melanie Farmer, with Master’s degrees in Biomimicry and Managing Innovation, each episode translates biological strategies into practical engineering insight, with a focus on building services.

title: Artifacts of Grief
author: Snack Labs
description:
Grief is a fundamental part of being human. Not an easy one, but an inevitable one. Artifacts of Grief is an intentional space of grief sharing. The objects around us are imbued with grief stories. They are tangible vessels of grief. Each episode begins with an object—an artifact of grief—that serves as a conduit to the grief story being shared. By bringing the artifact into  the physical space, grief becomes visible, something that can be felt, observed, held, both corporeally and emotionally. In sharing individual griefs, we honour both the singularity and shared threads of grief. We welcome the reality of this messy, complex, ongoing process through an invitation to witness. In sharing our stories of the griefs we live with, and the artifacts that represent them, we challenge the individualization of grief; instead, we understand grief as an experience that reveals the beauty and necessity of connection. Each artifact and each story weaves its thread into the collective grief gallery cultivated by Artifacts of Grief. Hosted by Kylie Burton and Elliott Kuss.


Artifacts of Grief is made by humans for humans, without the use of AI. 

Photography, artwork, editing, and original music by Elliott Kuss

Writing by Kylie Burton


Follow us on Instagram @artifactsofgrief

Hosted on Acast. See acast.com/privacy for more information.

title: I'm Just Thinkin'
author: Byron Harvey
description: A podcast encouraging people to ask Jesus into their...heads!

title: Christine's Home Affairs
author: Christine
description:
As my daily business is mostly related to sewing and second hand dealing, I'll be sharing with you my latest projects and ideas for sewing to sell. You'll learn to repair and alter clothes, construct new garments, and even upcycle some treasures from the Op Shops. As part of my second hand dealing, I've been enjoying ways to repurpose things long forgotten and unwanted, so you'll get to see what I'm doing with these previously unloved items. 
I love to get feedback, and am happy to try new things, so feel free to make requests for any video content that you'd like me to do.

title: The Other Owner
author: Alex
description: The Other Owner is a podcast about what it really takes to run a business with the person you share your life with. Hosted by Todd and Alex Hess, co-owners of a family painting company, each episode digs into the honest, messy, and rewarding reality of being business partners and life partners at the same time—dividing roles, navigating conflict, making big decisions together, and keeping both the company and the relationship healthy. Whether you're already running a business with your spouse or just curious how couples make it work, pull up a chair and hear it from the other owner.

title: Flagship
author: The Atlanta Journal-Constitution
description: AJC Flagship is your front-row seat to the Substance and Soul of the South. Each week, this show brings you a mix of what makes The Atlanta Journal-Constitution special. Here, the stories have a flavor all their own, and you never know where the conversation’s gonna go.

title: Hardloop Endurance Podcast
author: Hardloop Endurance
description: Join veteran coaches Kirk Elias and Cal Neff for weekly conversations covering the latest news in running, standout races, training philosophy, coaching insights, and everything in between. From elite performances to everyday runners, we share what we're learning from our athletes, what's shaping our coaching, and what's happening in our own lives—always with the goal of helping you become a stronger, smarter, and more resilient runner. Whether you're into track, road, trail, or ultrarunning, there's something here for every runner.

title: RatedRForREAL's Podcast
author: RatedRForREAL
description:
For the deep thinkers who enjoy thought-provoking conversations about a multitude of topics, you’ve stumbled across the right podcast. Rated R For REAL was designed with you in mind. Grab your favorite bottle of wine or make your favorite drink or cocktail, it’s about to get real- FAST! Each episode will leave you on edge. Topics will range from what’s trending worldwide across social media, relationships & marriage and more! Hosted by: Ashley B, D-SUUPPA,  and Kelvin Malone Jr. 


Special guests will join us on some episodes to get in on the discussions!

title: My Kind of Scene
author: Cara Diaria
description: My Kind of Scene uncovers the past and present of Australian music. From titans to hidden gems, this podcast discovers the songs and artists that make up the fabric of the Aussie music scene, and explores what makes them succeed. It examines history through a present-day lens, attempting to understand the giants whose shoulders today's artists stand on, and the creative sparks defining the future of Australian music. Host Cara Diaria, indie musician and music nerd, brings her unique perspective to the Aussie hits and misses, the movers and shakers, the goodness and greatness that makes it My Kind of Scene.

title: StoryWoven
author: Community Based Services
description:
Welcome to StoryWoven, a podcast created through an exciting partnership between Community-Based Services and Hudson Valley Interarts.
Hosted by Jon Harrison and enriched by guest hosts, this channel is a welcoming, compassionate, and uplifting space where stories are honored, voices are uplifted, and true community is built. Through the lens of creativity and lived experience, we explore the vital importance of inclusion, transition support, and the profound power of storytelling to bring people closer together.

title: Kids’ Health Explained
author: The Sydney Children’s Hospitals Network
description: A new podcast series hosted by Sophie Delezio, designed to connect parents and families with trusted, evidence-based information on children’s health.

title: Whose Body? A Lord Peter Wimsey Mystery
description: In the captivating debut of Dorothy L. Sayers's Lord Peter Wimsey series, a mysterious murder unfolds in an unexpected setting. An architect in Battersea stumbles upon a naked body in his bathtub, complete with a distinctive gold pince-nez. This shocking discovery sets off a chain of events that draws in the clever and aristocratic detective, Lord Peter Wimsey, along with his loyal manservant, Mervyn Bunter. As they delve into the investigation, they uncover a web of intrigue that challenges their wits and uncovers hidden motives. Scotland Yard’s Inspector Charles Parker also joins the fray, adding depth to the investigation. Set against the backdrop of 1920s London, this detective fiction classic combines sharp dialogue, vivid characters, and a gripping plot that keeps listeners guessing. Will Lord Wimsey solve the case before more secrets come to light? Join us for a journey filled with twists, turns, and the allure of vintage detective storytelling.

title: Most Days
author: Courtney Guillen
description: This is Most Days. I'm Courtney and I'm on a journey to become a better leader at work and at home, and share what I learn as I go.

title: The Rambling Historians
author: TheRamblingHistorians
description: A podcast dedicated to all things history with Dr Jojo Dickinson and Dr Peter Stiffell, two historians who love to ramble

title: Lesson Plans to Laces Podcast
author: Lesson Plans to Laces
description:
A podcast that blends sneaker culture and modern-day education topics.
Hosted by @teacherheat & @itsjalidor

title: The Bear Rugs Podcast
author: James Holden & Jeremy Lowe

description: Conversations on how AI is changing the way we work, featuring insights, ideas, and real-world lessons from the team behind Capably.

title: 5Nine Creative Podcast
author: 5Nine Creative
description: The 5Nine Podcast takes you behind the scenes of global gospel ministry through honest conversations, powerful stories, and the people making them happen. Join the team at 5Nine Creative as we explore visual storytelling, missions, creativity, and how God is using media to amplify the voices of those sharing the gospel around the world.

title: Intuitive Mastery
author: Christopher Early
description: This Podcast is here to assist you in elevating your consciousness, how to harness your intuition, and truly step into the magical essence of what’s possible to co-create with Source in all that is beheld.✨

title: Covenant Finder
author: More Than Conquerors
description:
Covenant Finder is a Christian podcast dedicated to exploring the raw, authentic, and spiritual journey of navigating relationships through the lens of faith.

Hosted by Minister and Prophet Jonathan Dorn, who brings nearly two decades of ministry experience, this show offers a uniquely transparent perspective on what it means to actively pursue God.Whether you are a single Christian preparing your heart for marriage or a married couple seeking deeper spiritual growth, Covenant Finder bridges the gap. Through raw, honest discussions and insightful interviews with couples who have achieved long-term marital success, Prophet Door tackles the real questions, challenges, and triumphs of both seasons of life.

Dive into a wealth of biblical knowledge and discover how to align your relationship status with God’s divine purpose.

title: Map the Gap
author: Grodivo.ai
description:
Strategy defines your destination, but alignment determines your speed. Yet, the root cause behind the failure of most corporate transformations, scaling milestones, and M&A initiatives isn't a flawed strategy—it is the invisible friction between people and execution.

Welcome to Map the Gap, the podcast that explores the critical, often-overlooked dimension of business: human alignment. Presented by Grodivo.ai, we move beyond subjective culture conversations to uncover the quantitative reality of how modern organizations actually collaborate, execute, and adapt.

In each episode, we sit down with renowned thought leaders, industry strategists, and subject matter experts to unpack the costly realities of workplace misalignment. We explore how bridging the strategy-execution gap turns organizational alignment into a measurable financial lever, making workplaces more fulfilling, efficient, collaborative, and highly profitable.

Whether you are a C-Suite executive driving a high-stakes shift, an HR leader engineering a resilient culture, an M&A practitioner protecting post-merger synergies, a CFO quantifying the ROI of people investments, or an individual employee wanting to see how your daily work connects to the big picture—Map the Gap™ provides the insights you need to eliminate friction, de-risk change, build trust, and empower your entire organization to move as one.

title: AI the Job Search: The Resume and Hiring Show
author: Equity Solutions Network
description:
The job search has changed more in the last two years than it did in the previous twenty. AI tools are everywhere. Job descriptions are confusing. ATS myths are out of control. And job seekers are stuck trying to figure out what's real, what's hype, and what actually gets them hired.
Hosted by Tyson McKay — CIO, CISSP, and founder of MyJobDaddy. Practical advice from someone who's hired hundreds of people and built AI tools to help you compete.
Try the AI resume tailoring tool free: https://myjobdaddy.com

title: The Path Forward
author: UiPath
description:
The Path Forward is a podcast from UiPath for leaders confronting one of the most important business questions of our time: how to turn AI momentum into something they can actually rely on. As AI moves into the center of enterprise strategy, the series explores the choices shaping the future of work: where human judgment matters, how industries will change, and what it takes to scale AI safely, openly, and accountably. 
With UiPath leaders, customers, and industry voices, each episode brings a grounded perspective on what this moment demands.
https://www.uipath.com/

title: Age Against the Machine
author: Wayne Senior Center
description: Age Against the Machine is a podcast dedicated to disrupting the narrative of getting older through real conversations. Featuring stories from community members, senior center participants, and experts, the podcast explores wellness, continued growth, caregiving, technology, and what it really means to live well as we grow older. Brought to you by the Wayne Senior Center.

title: The Next Play with Paul Andrew Ratner
author: Paul Ratner
description: The Next Play with Paul Andrew Ratner features conversations with former professional athletes, executives, entrepreneurs, and nonprofit leaders who have successfully navigated major career transitions. Host Paul Andrew Ratner explores the lessons learned after the spotlight fades, the business skills people wish they had learned earlier, and how successful leaders continue reinventing themselves throughout their careers.

title: Normal is a Lie
author: Julie Harris
description: A podcast where Julie Harris dismantles everything about what normal means, with history, science, lived experience and reality - not some fabricated version of it.

title: Ufedojo’s Perspective
author: Sandra Okolo
description: Sharing God’s love through personal stories, honest conversations, and everyday reflections on faith and life.

title: Trashy Genes
author: Steven and Jessica Werfel
description: Turns out trashy TV is genetic. Trashy Genes is a father-daughter podcast dedicated to the glorious chaos of reality television. Every week, we dive into the latest episodes of Love Island, Bravo favorites like Summer House, Vanderpump Rules, Southern Charm, and whatever else has us yelling at our TVs. Expect unfiltered opinions, hilarious debates, and plenty of laughs from two generations bonded by one guilty pleasure.

title: The Silent Alarm: Adventure on Pine Mountain
description: In 'Silent Alarm,' the sixth installment of the Adventures for Girls series, listeners follow Marion, a dedicated school teacher, as she embarks on a mission to uplift a struggling mountain community. Paired with the adventurous Florence Ormsby, Marion discovers the dilapidated state of the local school in the Cumberland Mountains of Eastern Kentucky. With a heart full of determination, the duo sets out to raise funds and use their talents to transform the educational landscape. Yet, they soon face resistance from some residents who are wary of change. This gripping tale explores themes of perseverance, friendship, and the power of education, as the girls navigate both the challenges and joys of their mission. Will they succeed in inspiring the community, or will their efforts be met with silence? 'Silent Alarm' offers a captivating mix of action and adventure, perfect for listeners seeking a story of courage and hope in the face of adversity.

title: The Practical Church Musician
author: The Practical Church Musician
description: A podcast dedicated to offering practical tools and ideas for volunteer and part-time church musicians everywhere. Each week, we explore the real-life joys and challenges of serving as a church musician, no matter your denomination, background, or experience. From developing your musical skills to managing the administrative side of a church music program, this show is here to support your growth and strengthen the work you do for your congregation.

title: Shining With ADHD by The Childhood Collective
author: The Childhood Collective
description:
Are you a parent of a child with ADHD (or suspected ADHD)? Are you overwhelmed by the treatment options to support your child? Are you exhausted from giving your child with ADHD *countless* reminders for the same tasks… yelling to get them to (sometimes) listen… and walking on eggshells to avoid the seemingly endless meltdowns? Are you getting constant calls from the school about your child’s behavior? Is your child struggling in school without a plan in place to help them? If you want to truly understand ADHD, find practical tools that work, and feel empowered to get your child the right supports at school, this show is for you.
The Shining with ADHD Podcast by the Childhood Collective is a live-workshop style educational podcast packed with actionable, step-by-step strategies, empathy, and of course, some humor! You can learn from the experts as you navigate this chaotic ADHD parenting journey, create calm in your home, and help your
child shine at school.
Your hosts Katie, Lori, and Mallory of The Childhood Collective have helped thousands of overwhelmed parents find joy and confidence in raising their child with ADHD. Katie is a speech language pathologist, and Lori and Mallory are both child psychologists. As busy mamas themselves, they are on a mission to combine the science of ADHD with the compassion of moms. Each week, they share inspiration and tips to help you tackle challenging behaviors, build executive functioning skills, and manage big emotions.
Along with sharing their best ADHD parenting advice and stories, The Childhood Collective interviews top ADHD professionals *and* relatable ADHD parents who share their favorite tools to empower you EVERY time you tune in. With their online community growing every day, now YOU can learn from the experts, get inspired, and raise a happy and independent child with ADHD!

title: 🎙️ Beyond the Voice Podcast
author: Yetta Mae Hansell
description:
Welcome to Beyond the Voice Podcast, hosted by Ms. Yetta and presented by Riverview Fine Arts Academy. This is more than a singing podcast—it's a journey of confidence, mindset, motivation, and personal growth. Each week, you'll discover vocal tips, performance coaching, and practical strategies to help you find your voice, overcome fear, and become the person and performer you were created to be.

If this podcast inspires you and you're ready to invest in your voice, I'd be honored to be part of your journey. At Riverview Fine Arts Academy, I help singers of all ages and experience levels build strong technique, lasting confidence, and a genuine love for singing.

title: Angel Foundation Podcast - Hosted by Liz Brewer
author: Angel Foundation FL
description:
For 20 years, Angel Foundation FL has walked alongside families facing life‑threatening illness or sudden crisis — offering hope, dignity, and practical support when it’s needed most. Now, the stories behind that mission are coming to life in a new way.


The Angel Foundation Podcast – Angels Among Us is hosted by CEO Liz Brewer, who sits down with the people who have shaped, served, and strengthened the foundation over the last two decades. From volunteers and business partners to Angel Team members, families, community leaders, and long‑time supporters, each episode highlights the individuals who make this mission possible.

This podcast isn’t about titles or spotlight moments. It’s about real people showing up for one another. It’s about compassion in action. It’s about the quiet, consistent, faithful work that has allowed Angel Foundation FL to support thousands of families through their hardest seasons.

Listeners will hear:


  • Stories of families impacted by crisis — and the angels who stepped in
  • Conversations with volunteers, donors, and partners who fuel the mission
  • Behind‑the‑scenes insights into how Angel Teams operate
  • Reflections on 20 years of community care and what comes next
  • The heart, history, and hope that define Angel Foundation FL


At its core, Angels Among Us is a celebration of community — a reminder that when people come together with compassion and purpose, lives change.


Angel Foundation Podcast – Angels Among Us  
Real stories. Real impact. Real angels walking among us.

title: Electricity Bills in Pakistan
author: LESCO eBill
description: Electricity bills in Pakistan are something we all deal with every month. Sometimes the bill is late, sometimes we lose it, and sometimes we just want to check the amount before the due date. In this episode, I have explained how you can check your LESCO bill online by using the reference number or customer ID. You can view units, due date, taxes, download duplicate bill, print it, and pay it easily through online payment methods. So if your bill is missing, don’t worry; this simple guide will help you check it fast.

title: JK Man Podcast
author: Dillon
description: A comedy podcast where I give my opinions and talk about a variety of topics. Anywhere from movies, video games, food,  music, and whatever else we can come up with along the way. This is just for fun and laughs. So just remember, don't take any of it too seriously, and besides, I was just kidding man!

title: CHANGE: SIMPLE CONCEPTS THAT WILL CHANGE YOUR LIFE FOREVER by Brad Young
author: Brad Young
description: Welcome to the C.H.A.N.G.E: Simple Concepts That Will Change Your Life Forever podcast, where we explore the transformative power of six key elements: Communication, Habits, Attitude, Network, Goals, and Education. Each episode delves into practical strategies and inspiring stories that illuminate how these concepts can be harnessed to create profound personal and professional growth. Join us as we engage with experts, share real-life experiences, and offer actionable insights that will empower you to embrace change and unlock your full potential. Whether you're looking to enhance your personal development or improve your career trajectory, this podcast is your guide to embracing C.H.A.N.G.E. and crafting the life you envision.

title: THE CONTEXT WINDOW
description:
The Context Window: Framing the future. Today.

You see the headlines. You see the viral moments. But what's the real story?

We're the talk show that moves beyond the buzz. Every week, host JULES and Resident Analyst JULIET dive deep into the forces driving the world's most fascinating cultural, economic, and technological shifts. We connect the dots between emerging micro-trends and global movements, giving you the data, context, and foresight you need to understand what's coming next.

Featuring our rapid-fire "The Pulse" segment and the high-stakes "Trend or End?" debate, this is the essential weekly download for the curious mind that wants to analyze the future, not just observe it.

title: Midlife FItness and Beyond Podcast
author: Richard & Hiro
description: "Welcome to Midlife Fitness and Beyond- the podcast for men and women aged 35–55 who are ready to reclaim their health, build real strength, and live with more energy and purpose. Hosted by Richard, a Strength & Conditioning Coach with over 30 years of experience, and Hiro, a qualified Holistic Health and Nutrition Coach and Pharmacy Technician, we combine science-backed training with holistic nutrition and lifestyle strategies to help you age strong. Whether you're dealing with fatigue, stress, or weight gain or simply want to perform better and feel great this is the show for you."

title: Witches Cove: Secrets of the Maine Coast
description: In the heart of historic Maine, two friends embark on an unforgettable summer filled with adventure and mystery. Ruth introduces Betty to the rugged beauty of the coastline, where fishing and clam digging intertwine with the thrill of dodging smugglers and navigating perilous explosives. As they explore the secrets of Witches Cove, their courage and cleverness are put to the test. Will their exceptional physical strength and quick thinking be enough to uncover the hidden dangers lurking beneath the surface? Join Ruth and Betty on this action-packed journey that blends friendship, exploration, and suspense. Perfect for young listeners eager for excitement and intrigue, 'Witches Cove' promises an engaging experience that sparks the imagination and celebrates the spirit of adventure.

title: Now is Your Yes
author: Darcy Peckskamp
description:
Hey friend—welcome to Now Is Your Yes.
This is the podcast for the person who knows they’re called to more… but feels stuck, uncertain, or afraid to take the next step.
Here, we don’t just talk about faith—we live it.
We don’t just dream about growth—we pursue it.
And we don’t sit on the sidelines—we take action.
Because your purpose isn’t someday.
It’s now.

title: Rusty Nail and Friends
author: Barry James Freeman
description: Rusty Nail and Friends is a gentle, character-led storytelling world where little things come to life, and big feelings feel safe. Here you will find calm, imaginative bedtime adventures that help children slow down, feel safe, and connect with meaningful themes through whimsical talking objects and nature-filled settings.  The stories were written by Granddad for his many grandchildren and now he reads them for children everywhere.  New stories each week.

title: Positive Charge by GeekWire
author: GeekWire
description:
Positive Charge by GeekWire explores the technology breakthroughs working to solve the planet's biggest environmental and sustainability challenges. Hosted by GeekWire's Lisa Stiffler and audio producer Laura Scott, each episode goes inside the labs, companies, and wild places of the Pacific Northwest — and beyond — to meet the creators, champions, and consumers bringing bold climate innovations to life.
This is a podcast about hope. The planet faces real challenges, but there are smart, passionate people working hard to address them — whether they're chasing clean energy, treating persistent "forever chemicals," or rethinking sustainability from the ground up. We profile those people and the breakthroughs giving us reason for optimism.
Brought to you by Amazon Sustainability.

title: Scene 69
author: The Chronicle Factory
description:
Scene 69 is a space for honest, long form conversations with remarkable people from all walks of life.

We believe that meaningful dialogue has the power to inspire curiosity, challenge assumptions, and help us see the world through a different lens. 

Our goal is simple. To create conversations that are thoughtful, authentic, and worth remembering. We ask questions, listen deeply, and explore the experiences, ideas, and values that shape the people behind them. Every episode is an opportunity to discover something new, reflect on different perspectives, and connect through genuine conversation.

title: Legacy Podcast - The Curated Life
author: Tendai Marowa- Radio Podcast
description:
Welcome to Legacy Podcast: The Curated Life Show—where style meets substance, culture inspires purpose, and every conversation leaves a lasting impact.
I'm your host, Tendai Marowa, inviting you into a space where visionary leaders, entrepreneurs, creatives, and changemakers share the stories, wisdom, and experiences shaping the future.
Together, we'll explore leadership, luxury, wellness, fashion, entrepreneurship, and the power of intentional living—all through conversations that inspire, empower, and elevate.
This is more than a podcast. It's a movement. This is Legacy Podcast. Let's begin."

title: Get Seen, Get Sales: The Threads Marketing Podcast
author: Taylor Lane
description:
The show where you learn the Threads marketing and sales strategies that Taylor Lane uses to get her clients found, followed, and paid DAILY.


If you're tired of feeling like the best-kept secret in your industry—working way too hard for the amount of people who actually know you exist—this podcast is about to change the way you think about marketing.


This is for coaches, course creators, and online service providers who already have an offer that works and are ready for more visibility, more qualified leads, and more sales... without spending all day creating content or chasing every new platform.


Welcome to Get Seen, Get Sales. I'm Taylor Lane, founder of a Done-for-You Threads agency that's helped online business owners generate daily leads, launch more successfully, and build marketing systems that keep working long after they hit publish.


Around here, we believe getting seen isn't about going viral. It's about becoming impossible for the right people to ignore.


Each episode, we're getting into things like:

  • How to use Threads to attract warm, ready-to-buy leads
  • Marketing strategies that create demand before you make an offer
  • Building a lead generation system instead of relying on launches
  • Real client case studies and behind-the-scenes marketing breakdowns
  • Messaging, positioning, and content that actually converts into sales
  • Honest conversations about what it really takes to build a business people can't stop talking about


Whether you're adding Threads to your marketing ecosystem or simply want more predictable growth, you're in the right place.


So grab your iced coffee, head out on your walk, or press play before your next work session—and let's build a business that gets seen and gets sales.


Want to come say hi?


Find me on Threads and Instagram @taylor.elizabethlane, or send me a DM and let me know you found me through the podcast. I'd love to hear what you're working on right now to Get Seen and Get Sales!

author: Samuel Agnew
description:
Every business has a blueprint. Behind every café, shop, restaurant, salon, workshop and local brand is a story of risk, resilience and determination. Hosted by entrepreneur Sam Agnew, The Blueprint takes listeners behind the scenes to uncover the real journeys of independent business owners who built something from nothing. Through honest conversations, we explore the highs, the lows, the lessons learned and the decisions that shaped their success. Whether you’re an entrepreneur, a small business owner, or simply fascinated by inspiring stories, every episode offers practical advice, genuine insight and powerful motivation from the people who keep our high streets alive. This is more than a business podcast—it’s a collection of the stories, struggles and blueprints behind extraordinary people building extraordinary businesses.

Hosted on Acast. See acast.com/privacy for more information.

title: The Stampede Ministries’ podcast
author: Stampede Ministries
description: Welcome to The Stampede Ministries Podcast, the official youth voice of FBC Shallowater. A stampede isn't just a group of Mustangs running—it’s a collective movement that shakes the ground and changes the landscape. That’s exactly what we’re chasing. Each week, we’re coming together to talk about what it looks like to live out an unstoppable, unbridled faith in our everyday lives. Tune in as we dive deep into real-life Bible studies, share hilarious youth group stories, and sit down with our youth leaders to hear their raw, unscripted testimonies of God’s faithfulness. Pull up a seat, hear the stories, and run with the herd. New episodes drop weekly!

And it all starts now….Welcome to the “Be Future Ready Podcast” — brought to you by Live In Your Strengths, Hosted by Sarah Price.

Faith isn't just for Sunday Mornings!
You Gotta Have Faith is a limited podcast series where I'll be exploring faith in the real world. I'll be examining current events, culture, technology and what it means to follow God in a rapidly changing world. Expect thoughtful conversations, personal reflections, guest testimonies and a healthy dose of curiosity . title: What Happened to Mary: A Mystery of Kidnapping and Betrayal
description: Mary Craig, the adored granddaughter of a wealthy patriarch, becomes the center of a chilling mystery when she is kidnapped at just four months old. Her grandfather, fiercely protective and paranoid, has dedicated his life to safeguarding her. With her parents gone and his other descendants disowned, he views Mary as his most precious treasure. However, everything changes when the unimaginable happens: she is taken away, and the kidnapper is killed before he can reveal her location. As time passes and with Mary’s fate hanging in the balance, her grandfather makes a drastic decision on his deathbed that could alter the family’s future forever. 'What Happened to Mary' unravels a tale of family loyalty, desperation, and the lengths one will go to protect loved ones. Can the truth about Mary’s disappearance be uncovered before it’s too late? Tune in to explore a gripping plot filled with twists, secrets, and the haunting question of what truly happened to Mary.

title: Make This Make Sense
author: MakeThisMakeSensePod
description: Make This Make Sense brings together three Entrepreneurship professors unpack the systems, trends, and social dynamics everyone is confused about — with the women who actually understand them. Guests include academics and entrepreneurs covering various industries diving into the latest research and sharing their experiences.

title: Get Truth
author: David
description: Have you ever wanted to know the truth? Maybe you're unsure if there's more than one truth or you might be curious at what truth is. Well you've come to the right place because I will be covering everything and anything that revolves around the truth and hopefully, to the best of my abilities, I will be able to set you free through the truth related to the truth which is in the Bible.

title: Inner Chambers with Meir Moshel
author: Meir Moshel
description:
Inner Chambers with Meir Moshel is a compassionate exploration of spirituality in the human experience.
Each episode is an invitation to enter the inner realms of the soul and encounter new perspectives on life's deeper questions.
Drawing inspiration from Jewish mystical traditions, Jungian psychology and the wisdom found in everyday life, these conversations create a space of reflection and possibility.
Welcome to Inner Chambers.
Questions and comments are always welcome at innerchamberswithmeir@gmail.com.

title: Amplified Whole Health - Where personal and organizational health meet
author: Justin Collier
description: Insightful interviews with business owners and leaders from a variety of industries.  Focused on: Clarity of Purpose, Employee Development, Leadership & Strategy, Culture,  Quality Products and Services, Communication, Customer Care & Shareholder Value

title: The Jerry Glazier Show
author: Mr. Glazier
description: This is a every day man, faith-driven podcast built around real conversations with people who have lived, built, struggled, served, lost, endured, and learned. Hosted by Jerry Glazier, the show explores Christian faith, work, family, leadership, human nature, resilience, purpose, and what really matters. Guests are not celebrities; they are contractors, tradesmen, veterans, ranchers, small-business owners, pastors, first responders, and everyday people with extraordinary experiences. Expect candid conversations, practical wisdom, and stories that help listeners outlast the temporary and hold onto what truly lasts.

title: The Devil Is Going To Hang
author: TDIGTH Podcast
description: The word of God says the Truth will set you free...however the truth has been hidden behind false ideology and pretense. Day after day, many are walking in a half truth reality. Never finding freedom, purpose, or destiny. In hopes of shattering the illusions that are presented as truth today we will tackle the controversial sides of faith and the ultimate Truth of a God who is real and still works in the lives of His children today through healing, miracles, and various signs and wonders. We don't just get a "golden ticket" for salvation and eternal life but a invitation to change the world.

title: The CrazyFitnessGuy® Show — Sensory Friendly Health & Autism Mental Health
author: Jimmy Clare | Neurodivergent Fitness & Sensory Friendly Health
description: The CrazyFitnessGuy® Show is a calm, judgement‑free podcast about healthy living through autistic eyes, created for neurodivergent adults who feel overwhelmed by typical fitness and wellness advice. Hosted by Jimmy Clare—autistic motivational speaker, autism advocate, and founder of CrazyFitnessGuy—you'll hear honest conversations about simple routines, mental health, sensory‑friendly health habits, and realistic fitness and nutrition that actually fit your life, not the other way around. Each episode features down‑to‑earth tips, lived‑experience stories, and expert guests who reject toxic fitness culture and "one size fits all" rules so you can build small, sustainable habits, reduce burnout, and create a calmer, healthier life at your own pace.AwardsBest Neurodivergent Focused Fitness & Wellness Platform 2026 - USAAwarded Speaker of the Year for Resilience 2026

description:
A thought leadership podcast that examines the relationship between identity, decision-making, and influence.
Who you are shapes what you do and the ripple extends further than you know. Identity is layered, and so is impact. This podcast explores how who we are drives influence, shapes decisions, and creates lasting effects across our lives, our work, and the world.

With your host, Stef Loisou, the financial practitioner who makes house calls.

author: Get Flushed Studio
description: The Motorcycle Advocacy Group New Zealand (MAGNZ) is an incorporated society that represents motorcyclists in New Zealand at all levels of government, regulatory agencies and media.  It advocates for rider safety, rights and community engagement, supporting affiliated motorcycle clubs and rider groups through consolation and collaboration.  The society promotes responsible motorcycling practices and lawful participation in motorcycle activities.

title: The Thirty-Nine Steps: A Thrilling Spy Adventure

description: This fourth in the five novels about the giants Pantagruel and his father Gargantua is the last novel indusputably attributed to François Rabelais. Their adventures provide many opportunities for satire, the most effective being against intolerance, credulity, and cowardice, with Pantagruel himself always affording a calm center of sanity and equilibrium. As the sea and the islands teem with monsters and madmen, so the style erupts: Nearly endless lists tumble out, nonsense words of 20-30 syllables challenge the tongue and baffle the mind, and recondite learning spills with hilarity onto the page. title: Genius
description: "The only figure of literary repute who ever rated The "Genius" as first among the novels of Theodore Dreiser was Theodore Dreiser," literary historian Larzer Ziff observed. His fifth published novel, The "Genius" was actually the third novel Dreiser began work on and, as his most autobiographical work, remained the novel closest to his heart. He worked on it in stages over a four-year period. The credit he felt he deserved (and did not receive) for his honesty about sexual urges and damaged relationships and his original publisher's decision not to stand by the novel in the face of criticism contributed to his lifelong feeling that the book had never been given its due. After An American Tragedy, it is his longest book; the final draft ran to over 700 pages in a close-set type. While the protagonist of the book is in many ways a portrait of its author, Dreiser also loosely based Eugene Witla on some of the painters, artists working in an Ashcan realist style, whom he knew in New York at the time and whose studios he visited. The most likely candidate for a model is Everett Shinn, who painted urban scenes of the kind attributed to Witla and who was known as a promiscuous man. The novel is divided intro three sections: "Youth," "Struggle," and "Revolt." In Book I, Eugene Witla (like Sister Carrie, in Dreiser's earlier novel) escapes the confines of the small town in Illinois where he has been raised to make his way in Chicago. There he studies painting at the Chicago Art Institute and enjoys the excitement of the city and his first sexual experiences. He becomes engaged to a young woman, Angela Blue, with whom he is intimate before their marriage but, at all times, he finds it difficult to remain faithful. A life based on monogamy seems beyond him. In Book II, Eugene and Angela move to New York City, where he makes a name for himself in the art world as an urban realist but finds his marriage with the increasingly conventional Angela painfully limiting. They travel to Europe, he suffers a breakdown, and they return to New York where Eugene attempts to make a better living in the advertising world. Book III chronicles the deterioration of Eugene and Angela's marriage as he begins an affair with Suzanne Dale. (Wikipedia)

title: Vanity Fair
description: Vanity Fair: A Novel without a Hero is a novel by William Makepeace Thackeray that satirizes society in early 19th-century England. Like many novels of the time, Vanity Fair was published as a serial before being sold in book form; it was printed in 20 monthly parts between January 1847 and July 1848. Thackeray meant the book to be not only entertaining but also instructive; this is shown both by the narrator of the book and in Thackeray's private correspondence. The novel is now remembered as a classic of English literature, though some critics claim that it has structural problems; Thackeray sometimes lost track of the huge scope of his work, mixing up characters' names and minor plot details. The number of allusions and references it contains can make it difficult for modern readers to follow.
